excluded: standalone auditability advantage beyond the robustness metric surface
post-hoc readout mitigation or threshold changes
supported: reduced-precision 2048-shot IBM ibm_fez hardware robustness advantage
fixed-width matched-comparator comparison across PhaseWrap, RoPE-like, sinusoidal-like, ALIBI-like, and matched nonzero null control
two independent IBM seed pairs and two circuit templates under preregistered Stage198 thresholds
calibration-applied q1q0 bitstring ordering before metric interpretation

next_gate: Use this as the reduced-scope hardware conclusion; pause before any full 4096-shot or cross-backend spend.
objective: Determine whether PhaseWrap-RoPE's compact phase-wrap positional score has measurable robustness or auditability advantages on noisy quantum hardware, compared with matched positional-score encodings, under fixed circuit width.
supported_claim: Under the preregistered reduced-precision fixed-width protocol on IBM ibm_fez, PhaseWrap-RoPE's compact phase-wrap positional score showed lower normalized noise sensitivity than matched RoPE-like, sinusoidal-like, ALIBI-like, and matched nonzero null-control encodings across two seeds and two circuit templates.
